text: [Music]
there is a vending machine for every 23
people in Japan but the highest vending
machine per capita on the planet
[Music]
after the business card Fiasco I started
to become keenly aware of all the
vending machines that I saw here in
Japan I noticed they are everywhere
indeed what we're looking at here is a
Japanese institution behind me sits an
entire shop dedicated to chopsticks yes
I'm about to go inside the first thing
you have to know in order to understand
the vending machine is that Japan is an
ageing country the average age here is
46 years old which is almost double the
world average and the paternity rate is
1.4 which means the population is
actually shrinking this is actually a
looming crisis for Japan generally but
one of the effects of it is that the
labor market is very expensive there's a
scarcity of low-skilled labor so instead
of paying a sales clerk to sit and
collect your money and when you buy a
piece of gum they just put it in a
machine and automate the whole thing and
the same goes for real estate Japan is
one of the densest countries in the
world 93 percent of the population live
in cities people literally live an
apartment smaller than your SUV so
instead of paying a lot of money for a
store front retailers will just slip a
little machine into an alleyway to save
a lot of money and they can still turn a
really good profit according to one
essay that I read from a Japanese
economist in Tokyo the bigger
explanation for the vending machines is
a fascination or even an obsession with
automation and robotics everything that
can be automated here is automated when
I go into order like a ramen or
breakfast more often than not i order on
a machine and I give a little ticket to
someone it can dictate of a broader
cultural trend of wanting to automate
every system you possibly can
[Music]
every taxi in Tokyo has automated doors
of the driver controls though now i
don't want to overstate them there's
still a major appreciation for
handcrafted artisanal goods here in
japan a good example of this is the
seven-year-old coffee shop i just got
out of where they literally use a
weighted scale to weigh their coffee
beans before grinding them and brewing
them to order
[Music]
to cool down their coffee they put it
into a metal vessel and spin it around a
giant ice cube so yes they love
automation but they're still very much
in touch with handmade so another thing
that totally contributes is this coinage
so much coinage the one big caveat to
the whole automation thing is that
definitely gotten on board with credit
cards yet everything is cash base and
because that you always have to clean it
when the highest coin is worth like five
dollars and let's be honest there's
nothing more satisfying than unloading
some of the change in your pocket into a
vending machine for some yummy treat my
personal favorite item this hot green TV
comes out wonderfully warm and you just
wonder how you got so lucky so Japan is
an ageing nation with expensive labor
and a love for robots and too many coins
in its pocket
[Music]
